title: Morgan Stanley Financial Summary From 2025 Form 10-K
text: Morgan Stanley reported full-year 2025 net revenues of $70.6 billion, up 14% from $61.8 billion in 2024. Net income applicable to Morgan Stanley was $16.9 billion, up 26% from $13.4 billion, and diluted EPS increased 28% to $10.21. The firm delivered return on equity of 16.6%, return on tangible common equity of 21.6%, and an expense efficiency ratio of 68%, compared with 71% in the prior year. Institutional Securities generated $33.1 billion of net revenues, driven by stronger equity activity and higher investment banking advisory and underwriting revenue. Wealth Management produced $31.8 billion of net revenues, supported by higher asset management revenue, strong fee-based flows, and $356 billion of net new assets. Investment Management generated $6.5 billion of net revenues, mainly from higher asset management fees on higher average AUM. At year-end 2025, Morgan Stanley had $1.42 trillion of total assets and $9.276 trillion of client assets. Key risks include market volatility, lower deal activity, credit losses, regulatory requirements, liquidity risk, operational risk, cybersecurity, litigation, and interest-rate sensitivity.
